open_positions.name: Ducommun Incorporated
open_positions.thesis: no mechanism. Catalyst: Defense sector pullback after positive earnings reaction faded. Falsified by: n/a.
open_positions.latest_reasoning: UNDERDONE conf 3 entry on defense sector pullback 7 days ago ('days' horizon). Position is -3.17% unrealized. Thesis was that pullback was overdone and cyclical. However, today's new flags (KTOS, AEIS) show defense actually rallying on Iran escalation, which is the opposite setup from 7 days ago when I bought the dip. The pullback narrative appears dated. Thesis is weakening but not yet broken — defense positioning is fluid and sector could fade again tomorrow if Iran de-escalates. Hold through short horizon expiry (expected tomorrow/next session) rather than sell at -3% realized; if sector reverses tomorrow, thesis may re-inflate.
open_positions.catalyst: Defense sector pullback after positive earnings reaction faded

open_positions.name: Builders FirstSource, Inc.
open_positions.thesis: no mechanism. Catalyst: Multi-item 8-K (5.02 officer change + 7.01 Reg FD) coinciding with 30-yr yield spike and mortgage rate surge. Falsified by: n/a.
open_positions.latest_reasoning: UNDERDONE conf 3 entry 3 days ago on 8-K + mortgage rate spike. Position is +7.29% unrealized. NEW catalyst has fired: House approved housing affordability bill TODAY (explicitly framed as 'win for investors'; removed build-to-rent industry opposition). BLDR is direct beneficiary as largest building-products supplier. New flag cites move on only 1.02x volume despite meaningful policy catalyst, meaning housing-bill news not yet absorbed by funds. This is genuinely new information (bill passage today) that did not exist at 3-day-old entry. Thesis is strengthening: original 8-K + mortgage tailwind plus now explicit policy win. Confidence remains 3 (policy needs Senate passage, but House passage is material diffusion opportunity). ADD sizing: 6% per exploratory tier (original entry was exploratory UNDERDONE conf 3). Current position ~9 shares at 74.15 = ~$666 notional (~6.5% of $10k bankroll). Add 6% = ~$600 more, ~8 shares at current price.
open_positions.catalyst: Multi-item 8-K (5.02 officer change + 7.01 Reg FD) coinciding with 30-yr yield spike and mortgage rate surge

open_positions.name: Deckers Outdoor Corporation
open_positions.thesis: no mechanism. Catalyst: Q4/FY earnings release filed via 8-K item 2.02. Falsified by: n/a.
open_positions.latest_reasoning: UNDERDONE conf 3 entry today (0 days held) on Q4/FY earnings 8-K. Position is already +8.28% unrealized (up $106.67 from cost basis $98.45). New flag re-lists same position as UNDERDONE conf 3 with identical earnings catalyst, noting 5-day cumulative move of +8.06% is 'modestly above today's print' and suggesting 'follow-through over days as analysts revise.' Thesis is intact but entry has already captured the immediate earnings pop. New flag's 'what_confirms' (sell-side upgrades over 48h, segment re-acceleration, guidance raise, margin expansion) and 'what_kills' (guide maintained/lowered, segment deceleration, inventory build, tariff escalation) are valid. Position is small (6 shares, ~$640 notional, 6.4% of bankroll). Hold through the next 2 days to let sell-side upgrade cycle fire as new flag thesis predicts. If upgrades materialize with continued volume, position can run. If guidance maintenance/deceleration surfaces or tariff warnings appear, thesis breaks and exit is warranted. For now, thesis intact, upside still available.
open_positions.catalyst: Q4/FY earnings release filed via 8-K item 2.02

closed_positions.name: Advanced Energy Industries
closed_positions.exit_reasoning: Re-evaluation required: original entry was OVERDONE conf 3 on 8-K surprise, closed 4 days ago at -6.48% realized loss. New flag re-lists AEIS as OVERDONE conf 3 with 1.01/2.03/3.02 financing signature and sector beta narrative. However, I explicitly exited this position 4 days ago because thesis was killed: the move was pure semicap-sector beta (1.6x SMH), not idiosyncratic catalyst, and conviction was gone. The new flag argues financing terms (convert premium, use of proceeds) could be priced in—plausible, but it is the SAME 8-K event I already saw on exit day (timestamp 5/13), and the re-entry bar is NOT met: confidence is still 3 (re-entry requires ≥4), and there is NO genuinely new information (the 8-K itself was the prior-exit catalyst; flag just re-describes it with added thesis flavor about financing terms). Thesis was killed by sector beta; flag does not supply new information to resurrect it. Exit to stop further drift and redeploy cash.

closed_positions.name: Advanced Energy Industries, Inc
closed_positions.exit_reasoning: Original entry was OVERDONE conf 3 on 8-K surprise narrative. New flag reclassifies as RATIONAL conf 3 and provides 8-K URL (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/927003/000110465926060543/aeis-20260513x8k.htm) confirming two consecutive 8.01 filings are 'likely investor conference / presentation materials'—benign. Thesis killed: the move (-6.13% new vs -7.96% realized) is pure sector beta (1.6x SMH), not an idiosyncratic catalyst. Position down -7.96% after 2 days with no directional edge remaining. Conviction gone; exit to redeploy capital.

closed_positions.name: Extreme Networks
closed_positions.exit_reasoning: Entered on thesis that EXTR was experiencing sympathy selling off CALX's -14% print—a short-term dislocations trade with 'days' horizon. 8 days in, EXTR has recovered +19% while XLK remains elevated, indicating the read-through trade has normalized and the catalyst (CALX weakness dragging peers) has exhausted its effect. Holding further exposes the portfolio to reversal risk without a fresh catalyst. The original high-confidence short-term mismatch has been arbitraged away. No evidence of broader enterprise IT weakness that would sustain further upside. Exit captures the gain and redeploys capital toward fresh opportunities.
